nodes.title: Sets and Functions
nodes.summary: The two foundational objects: collections and rules that map elements between them.

nodes.title: Logic and Proof
nodes.summary: Propositional and predicate logic, truth tables, the basic proof strategies (direct, contrapositive, contradiction, induction).

nodes.title: Counting and Combinatorics
nodes.summary: Counting principles: permutations, combinations, the pigeonhole principle, inclusion-exclusion.

nodes.title: Relations and Orderings
nodes.summary: Equivalence relations, partial and total orders, equivalence classes.

nodes.title: Groups, Rings, Fields
nodes.summary: Abstract algebraic structures with binary operations satisfying axioms.

nodes.title: Vector Spaces
nodes.summary: Sets closed under linear combinations over a field; the universal object of linear algebra.

nodes.title: Linear Maps and Matrices
nodes.summary: Structure-preserving functions between vector spaces; the matrix representation.

nodes.title: Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors
nodes.summary: Directions preserved by a linear map up to scaling; the building blocks of spectral analysis.

nodes.title: Inner Product Spaces
nodes.summary: Vector spaces equipped with a notion of angle and length via an inner product.

nodes.title: Norms and Metrics
nodes.summary: Generalised notions of distance; the foundation of analysis on vector spaces.

nodes.title: Sequences and Limits
nodes.summary: Convergence of sequences; the epsilon-N definition; Cauchy sequences and completeness.

nodes.title: Continuity
nodes.summary: The epsilon-delta definition; continuity as preservation of limits; uniform vs pointwise continuity.

nodes.title: Derivatives (Univariate)
nodes.summary: The local linear approximation; the limit of a difference quotient; differentiation rules.

nodes.title: Multivariate Calculus
nodes.summary: Partial derivatives; the chain rule in multiple variables; directional derivatives.

nodes.title: Gradient and Jacobian
nodes.summary: The gradient as the vector of partial derivatives; the Jacobian as the matrix of partial derivatives of a vector-valued function.

nodes.title: Integration (Riemann)
nodes.summary: The Riemann integral as a limit of step-function approximations; the fundamental theorem of calculus.

nodes.title: Series and Convergence
nodes.summary: Convergence of infinite sums; absolute vs conditional; power series and Taylor expansions.

nodes.title: Sample Spaces
nodes.summary: The set of all possible outcomes of an experiment; the starting point of probability.

nodes.title: Events and Sigma-Algebras
nodes.summary: A sigma-algebra is a collection of subsets closed under complement and countable union; the rigorous basis for which events can be assigned probabilities.

nodes.title: Probability Measures
nodes.summary: A function from a sigma-algebra to [0,1] satisfying countable additivity and P(Omega) = 1.

nodes.title: Conditional Probability
nodes.summary: P(A | B) = P(A and B) / P(B); the update rule for new information.

nodes.title: Independence
nodes.summary: P(A and B) = P(A) P(B); events that do not influence each other.

nodes.title: Random Variables
nodes.summary: Measurable functions from the sample space to the real line; the bridge from sets-of-outcomes to numerical values.

nodes.title: Probability Distributions
nodes.summary: The full description of a random variable: its cumulative distribution function, its discrete probability mass function, or its continuous probability density function.

nodes.title: Probability Density Functions
nodes.summary: A non-negative integrable function whose integral over a set gives the probability that a continuous random variable lands in that set.

nodes.title: Expectation
nodes.summary: The weighted average of a random variable's values, weighted by their probability; the integral of the random variable against its distribution.

nodes.title: Variance and Covariance
nodes.summary: Second-order moments measuring spread (variance) and linear association (covariance).

nodes.title: Conditional Expectation
nodes.summary: The expected value of a random variable given the value of another (or given a sub-sigma-algebra); the foundation of regression and martingale theory.

nodes.title: Change of Variables (Probability)
nodes.summary: How densities transform under invertible smooth maps; the Jacobian determinant appears as the volume-correction factor.

nodes.title: Ordinary Differential Equations
nodes.summary: Equations relating a function to its derivatives; the basic objects of dynamical systems.

nodes.title: Existence and Uniqueness (Picard-Lindelöf)
nodes.summary: Sufficient conditions (Lipschitz) under which an ODE has a unique solution; the foundation of well-posed dynamical systems.

nodes.title: Stochastic Processes
nodes.summary: Indexed families of random variables; the time-evolution of randomness.

nodes.title: Brownian Motion / Wiener Process
nodes.summary: The canonical continuous-time stochastic process with independent Gaussian increments; the building block of stochastic calculus.

nodes.title: Stochastic Differential Equations
nodes.summary: Differential equations driven by Brownian motion; the continuous-time analogue of random walks.

nodes.title: Itô Calculus
nodes.summary: The calculus of stochastic integrals where the integrator is non-smooth (Brownian); Itô's lemma replaces the chain rule.

nodes.title: Self-Information (Surprisal)
nodes.summary: I(x) = -log p(x); the surprise associated with observing an outcome under a probability distribution.

nodes.title: Shannon Entropy
nodes.summary: H(X) = E[-log p(X)]; the average information content of a random variable; measures uncertainty.

nodes.title: Cross-Entropy
nodes.summary: H(p, q) = -E_p[log q]; the average code-length when using distribution q to encode samples drawn from p.

nodes.title: KL Divergence
nodes.summary: D_KL(p || q) = E_p[log p - log q]; the relative entropy / information gain in moving from q to p. Non-symmetric, non-metric.

nodes.title: Mutual Information
nodes.summary: I(X; Y) = D_KL(p(x,y) || p(x) p(y)); the reduction in uncertainty about X from observing Y.

nodes.title: Information Geometry (Fisher Metric)
nodes.summary: Treats probability distributions as points on a Riemannian manifold; the Fisher information matrix as the natural metric tensor.

nodes.title: Optimal Transport (Wasserstein)
nodes.summary: Wasserstein distance W_p(mu, nu) = inf over couplings of E[|X - Y|^p]^(1/p); the cost of optimally moving one distribution to another. Provides a meaningful metric on distributions even when they have disjoint supports.
